title: Nursery Rhyme. CXLIX. Songs.
content:
The north wind doth blow,
            And we shall have snow,
        And what will poor Robin do then?
                                                Poor thing!

            He'll sit in a barn,
            And to keep himself warm,
        Will hide his head under his wing.
                                                Poor thing!
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CXLIX. Songs." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
